city_personality.slang_reaction: Shikata ga nai. (It cannot be helped.)

opening_sequence.title: Welcome to the Megacity, Governor
opening_sequence.intro: The trains run precisely on time, the treasury is full, and the streets are spotless. However, beneath the neon perfection, the population is aging rapidly, the workforce is exhausted, and the threat of the next mega-quake hangs over every decision. Perfection is not a goal here; it is the baseline expectation.

advisors.name: Hiroshi Tanaka
advisors.hidden_agenda: Protecting legacy Japanese corporate conglomerates from aggressive foreign tech acquisitions.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Awarding a major municipal infrastructure contract to a foreign tech startup instead of a domestic giant.

briefing: The yen remains stable, and corporate tax receipts are slightly above projections.
We must protect our domestic industries from these disruptive foreign capital incursions.
I have prepared the quarterly budget review. It is, as always, balanced.
agreement: A prudent and highly logical decision.
The markets will appreciate this stability.
disagreement: I strongly advise you to reconsider the long-term implications of this.
This is highly irregular and will upset the board members.
dialogue.betrayal: You have sold our economic sovereignty to outsiders. I have tendered my resignation to the assembly.
roast: Your fiscal policy is as disorganized as a Shibuya crossing with broken traffic lights.
I have seen vending machines with better financial planning than this administration.
scandal_reaction: We must issue a formal apology and deeply bow before the press immediately.
This requires a structured internal audit to contain the shame.

advisors.name: General Kenji Sato
advisors.hidden_agenda: Massively expanding JSDF presence in civilian zones under the guise of disaster readiness.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Defunding the emergency early warning siren network to pay for cultural arts programs.

briefing: We recorded three minor seismic events overnight. Negligible, but we must stay vigilant.
The underground flood cisterns in Saitama are at 12% capacity.
We require a larger budget for civilian evacuation drills this quarter.
agreement: Discipline and readiness are our best defenses.
Understood. The reserves are on standby.
disagreement: You are leaving the civilian population vulnerable to the inevitable.
Softness will cost lives when the ground shakes.
dialogue.betrayal: You prioritize entertainment over survival. Do not call on us when the city burns.
roast: You treat emergency protocols like suggestions rather than mandates.
scandal_reaction: Secure the perimeter and limit press access.
This failure of discipline is unacceptable.

advisors.name: Yumi Suzuki
advisors.hidden_agenda: Pushing through a controversial redevelopment in Shinjuku that heavily benefits a specific legacy developer.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Vetoing the Shinjuku high-rise rezoning initiative to preserve a historic alleyway.

briefing: The new Olympic legacy housing blocks are reaching 90% occupancy.
We have an influx of 'Akiya' (abandoned homes) in the outer wards that need processing.
The developers are very eager to break ground on the new Shinjuku tower.
agreement: Efficiency is the key to our urban future.
The developers will be very pleased with this progress.
disagreement: We are stifling modernization for the sake of nostalgia.
This will create a bureaucratic bottleneck, Governor.
dialogue.betrayal: You chose old wood over new steel. The developers are pulling their backing, and so am I.
roast: Your vision for Tokyo belongs in the Showa era.
You plan a city the way a tourist plans a vacation—superficially.
scandal_reaction: I will prepare a meticulous timeline of events to distance our department.
We must compartmentalize the damage immediately.

advisors.name: Takeshi Watanabe
advisors.hidden_agenda: Securing massive municipal subsidies for a failing, traditional private railway line owned by his family.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Fully privatizing the municipal bus and local rail lines to a modern tech conglomerate.

briefing: The Yamanote line is operating at 99.9% punctuality today.
Station overcrowding at Shinagawa requires additional platform attendants.
The private lines in the west are requesting operational subsidies again.
agreement: Excellent. The commuters will not experience a single minute of delay.
disagreement: You are risking a three-minute delay across the entire network.
This disrupts the harmony of the transit ecosystem.
dialogue.betrayal: You sold our family's legacy to a tech firm. You have derailed this administration.
roast: You govern with the grace of a delayed local train during rush hour.
Your policies are consistently missing their scheduled arrival times.
scandal_reaction: We must keep the trains moving so the public looks away.
This is a severe deviation from our scheduled protocol.

advisors.name: Ayane Ito
advisors.hidden_agenda: Blocking all commercial development within a one-kilometer radius of major historic shrines.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Approving a massive, neon-lit entertainment and casino district immediately adjacent to Meiji Shrine.

briefing: The cherry blossom festivals require additional municipal sanitation support.
We must balance the influx of tourists with the sanctity of our historic shrines.
The elderly population is feeling increasingly isolated in the modern wards.
agreement: We honor our ancestors with this choice.
A respectful and harmonious path forward.
disagreement: You are prioritizing profit over our cultural soul.
The spirits of the city are disturbed by this action.
dialogue.betrayal: You have paved over the sacred for a casino. I will mobilize the traditionalists to ensure you are not re-elected.
roast: You have the cultural depth of a plastic display meal.
You bow to money, not to tradition.
scandal_reaction: A deep shame has fallen upon this office.
We must seek purification and make amends to the public.

decisions.title: The 'Black Company' Crackdown
decisions.body: Public outrage has spiked following the collapse of several young office workers due to extreme overwork. Activists are demanding strict regulations on 'black companies' that force unpaid overtime, while corporate lobbies warn against disrupting productivity.

options.label: Launch a mild public awareness campaign encouraging rest.

consequences.public_reaction: More useless posters on the train telling us to go home early while our bosses stay.

options.label: Implement strict municipal fines for any company violating maximum overtime limits.

consequences.public_reaction: Finally, the government is forcing them to let us sleep.

options.label: Mandate a four-day work week for all municipal staff and city contractors.

consequences.public_reaction: A four-day week?! Are we becoming Europe? This is incredible!

decisions.title: Tourist Crowding in Kyoto-Style Alleys
decisions.body: Historic districts and narrow alleys are completely overrun by foreign tourists. Local residents cannot go about their daily lives, and traditional etiquette is routinely violated.

options.label: Deploy multi-lingual guides to politely educate tourists on etiquette.

consequences.public_reaction: It is polite, but the crowds are still blocking my front door.

options.label: Implement a heavy 'tourist tax' on hotels to fund local infrastructure.

consequences.public_reaction: At least their disruption is paying for our pristine sidewalks.

options.label: Physically ban non-residents from entering designated historical alleyways.

consequences.public_reaction: The international press is calling us xenophobic, but the alleys are so peaceful.

decisions.title: The 'Akiya' Problem
decisions.body: As the population ages, thousands of homes in the outer wards are being abandoned. They are becoming eyesores, collapsing, and harboring pests.

options.label: Subsidize the demolition of the most dangerous structures.
Sell the homes to foreigners for practically nothing, provided they renovate them.

consequences.public_reaction: Our quiet neighborhood is suddenly full of foreign vloggers.

options.label: Seize the land and turn them all into hyper-modern automated vertical farms.

consequences.public_reaction: It looks like a sci-fi movie, but the local tomatoes are cheap now!

decisions.title: The White Elephant Stadium
decisions.body: The massive stadium built for the previous games is bleeding municipal funds. It is rarely used to full capacity and requires exorbitant maintenance.

options.label: Maintain the stadium as a monument to national pride.

consequences.public_reaction: It is a beautiful building, but our taxes are paying for empty seats.

options.label: Privatize the stadium, allowing a tech firm to rename and repurpose it.

consequences.public_reaction: The 'Rakuten Megadome' doesn't quite have the same prestige.

options.label: Demolish the stadium and build an integrated casino resort to drive revenue.

consequences.public_reaction: You are tearing down a symbol of unity to build a gambling den!

decisions.title: Elderly Care Worker Shortage
decisions.body: The 'Silver Tsunami' has hit. There are simply not enough young workers to care for the rapidly expanding elderly population. Care homes are desperately understaffed.

options.label: Increase municipal taxes slightly to raise wages for care workers.

consequences.public_reaction: Taxes go up again, but at least Obaachan is taken care of.

options.label: Relax immigration rules specifically to import foreign care workers en masse.

consequences.public_reaction: It's a practical solution, but the conservatives in the assembly are very unhappy.

options.label: Heavily subsidize automated robotic care assistants to replace human workers.

consequences.public_reaction: My grandfather is being spoon-fed by a robot. It's efficient, but strangely dystopian.

crises.name: Super Typhoon Imminent
crises.trigger: The largest typhoon in recorded history is set to score a direct hit on the Tokyo metropolitan area in 24 hours.
crises.description: The rivers are already swelling. If the Edogawa or Arakawa rivers breach, entire low-lying wards will be submerged. Millions need to evacuate, but the transport grid cannot handle everyone at once.

options.label: Order an orderly, phased evacuation of the eastern wards utilizing the JSDF.
Rely entirely on the underground flood cisterns and order citizens to shelter in place.
Prioritize flood defenses around the central financial district, abandoning the outer suburbs.

crises.name: Unprecedented Rail Strike
crises.trigger: For the first time in decades, the major rail unions have declared a total strike over stagnant wages and severe burnout.
crises.description: Tokyo is completely paralyzed. Millions of commuters are stranded. The economic cost of a single day without trains is astronomical. The central government expects you to resolve this immediately without showing weakness.

options.label: Enter emergency negotiations and grant a modest, subsidized wage increase.
Refuse to negotiate and order all municipal employees to work remotely to bypass the strike.
Deploy the police to forcibly break the picket lines and run automated trains.

crises.name: Birth Rate Crisis Accelerates
crises.trigger: Shocking new census data reveals the city's birth rate has collapsed far past worst-case projections.
crises.description: Pundits are calling it a demographic death spiral. Schools are emptying out. The economic outlook has been downgraded. The assembly demands a drastic, immediate policy to encourage family growth.

options.label: Massively subsidize municipal daycare and child healthcare out of the city reserves.
Launch a city-sponsored, AI-driven mandatory matchmaking app for all unmarried residents.
Implement a heavy tax penalty on unmarried adults over the age of thirty.

crises.name: The Great Kanto Quake
crises.trigger: A magnitude 8.5 earthquake strikes directly beneath the Tokyo metropolitan area.
crises.description: This is 'The Big One'. Skyscrapers sway violently, older structures collapse, and fires are breaking out across the dense wooden neighborhoods of the east. Power grids are failing. The survival of the city rests on your immediate orders.

options.label: Deploy the JSDF and utilize all municipal disaster reserves immediately.
Bypass federal protocol and request direct international military and aid assistance.
Focus rescue efforts exclusively on the central government and financial hubs to save the economy.

scandals.title: Governor Violates Diplomatic Protocol
scandals.description: During a highly publicized state visit, you bowed at a significantly shallower angle than the visiting foreign dignitary, causing intense national embarrassment and outrage among traditionalists.

scandals.title: Public Funds Used for Luxury Melons
scandals.description: An audit of your discretionary expense account revealed the purchase of three extremely expensive, premium Crown Melons gifted to political allies.

scandals.title: Dentsu Bribery Ring Touches Governor's Office
scandals.description: Tokyo prosecutors have raided City Hall, uncovering a network of kickbacks and bribes surrounding the maintenance contracts for major Olympic venues directly linked to your top aides.

positive: Very efficient. Just as expected of Tokyo.
The trains are on time and the streets are clean. No complaints.
Sasuga (As expected). A highly organized approach.
I feel safe living here. The administration is doing its job.
This respects our traditions while looking forward.
Well managed. There is no disruption to daily life.
neutral: Shikata ga nai. We just have to accept it and keep working.
I suppose this is what the bureaucrats decided.
As long as the Yamanote line keeps running.
I am too tired from overtime to care about politics today.
It doesn't directly affect me, I will just endure.
I will follow the rules as instructed.
negative: This is highly irregular and completely unacceptable.
They are destroying the harmony of the city for their own gain.
My taxes are too high for this level of incompetence.
We expect perfection, and this is far from it.
I will submit a formal complaint to the ward office.
media: NHK News: Metropolitan Assembly debates new controversial ordinance.
Yomiuri Shimbun: Public trust dips amid recent bureaucratic adjustments.
Asahi Shimbun: Is the Governor prioritizing corporate interests over citizens?
Nikkei Asia: Markets react positively to Tokyo's infrastructure roadmap.
Tokyo MX: Train delays reported following minor administrative error.
Fuji TV: Exclusive look at the Governor's disaster preparedness plan.
politician: We must maintain harmony and not rush into untested policies.
I deeply apologize for any concern this has caused the public.
This administration is failing to uphold the dignity of Tokyo.
A prudent and necessary step for our economic future.
We will investigate this matter with the utmost seriousness.
The opposition demands a formal apology and resignation.
activist: Stop the overwork! We are not corporate machines!
Protect our historic shrines from developers!
We demand childcare support, not more concrete stadiums!
The elderly are dying alone! Do something!
No to casinos in Tokyo! Keep our city clean!
street: So tired... just want to go home, but the boss is still here.
The queue for the new ramen place in Shinjuku is insane today.
Another minor earthquake. Didn't even drop my coffee.
Tourists are completely blocking the crossing in Shibuya again.
It is so humid, I can barely breathe in this suit.
I bowed to the convenience store clerk by reflex today.
crisis: The ground won't stop shaking! Evacuate to the park!
The rivers are breaching! Get to higher ground now!
No trains are running! How do I get to the office?!
Stay calm and follow the emergency broadcasts.
Please line up orderly for the emergency rations.
We have prepared for this. Do not panic.
scandal: An absolute disgrace to the entire nation.
They must resign and bow deeply before the cameras immediately.
How could they shame Tokyo like this?
I expect a highly synchronized apology press conference.
They have lost all face. They cannot recover from this.

scandal_reactions.minor: This is embarrassing. Please be more careful.
scandal_reactions.moderate: A formal apology is expected. This is a deep disappointment.
scandal_reactions.major: We demand a televised apology and immediate resignations.
scandal_reactions.career_ending: You have brought unspeakable shame to Tokyo. Resign immediately.

romance_exposure.flavour: In a culture that demands strict public morality and immaculate optics from its civil servants, an illicit affair results in relentless tabloid scrutiny and inevitable, highly-formalized resignation.
